content.text:
Paste this into the cerebro Claude Code session in `/home/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/`.

---

# Per-IP form cross-platform verification — cerebro side

## Background

`Memos/memo-20260512-sentry-entry-port-repair-anchor.md` carries the canonical Fix Recipe — read the TL;DR and Fix Recipe sections before starting. macOS just landed the per-IP RETURN short-circuit form (the prior whole-CIDR form is now superseded) and verified three-fixture green:
- srjcl 3/3 ✓, pluml 5/5 ✓, tadmor (pending at time of dispatch — should be 54/54 if mirroring prior macOS green under Option E corrected).

Per-IP form is empirically pending on linux Docker Engine; whole-CIDR previously **FAILED** on this host (PREROUTING DNAT fired 0 times because the host's bridge-gateway IP `10.242.2.1` is inside the enclave CIDR). The hypothesis: per-IP exclusion of just the two enclave container IPs (sentry + bottle) allows the bridge gateway to pass naturally.

## State assumed at start

This worktree is currently in detached HEAD at `6102ed209` plus three throwaway local commits (`linux-local kludge srjcl/pluml/tadmor`) layered on top, with rbrn.env hallmarks pointing at old local kludge images. Those commits become invalid once we pull — the new sentry image will have a different hallmark.

Credentials state: `/home/bhyslop/projects/station-files/secrets/director/rbra.env` and `.../retriever/rbra.env` should already point at the `cancbhm-d-canest2bhm100001` project (copied over from macOS earlier this session). If not, the kludge will work fine (it's local) but the fixture's bottle-image auto-summon won't; address only if a fixture fails on a GAR pull.

## Procedure

### Step 1 — Discard throwaway local commits and move to the new pushed commit

```bash
git status -sb
# Expect: detached HEAD with 3 throwaway commits.

git fetch origin pym-₢A_AAP
# The new HEAD should be commit 593b69ef ("Refresh tadmor sentry hallmark to k260512111528-64aab808 (per-IP form)").

git checkout --detach origin/pym-₢A_AAP
git log -3 --oneline
# Confirm HEAD is the per-IP form commit. The throwaway local commits are unreferenced and will GC eventually.

grep -A 12 'RBJp2c: Configuring entry-port DNAT' rbev-vessels/common-sentry-context/rbjs_sentry.sh | head -20
# Confirm: should show THREE iptables rules (two -j RETURN then unconditional DNAT), not the prior single ! -s ENCLAVE_CIDR rule.
```

### Step 2 — Re-kludge sentry vessel + refresh all three nameplate hallmarks

The vessel kludge rebuilds the sentry image locally with the per-IP form. The per-nameplate KludgeSentry calls update each `.rbk/<np>/rbrn.env` to point at the new local image; each one needs a local commit before the next can proceed (kludge guard refuses dirty trees). These are throwaway commits on detached HEAD, fine to use plain `git commit --no-verify`.

```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"

./tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh rbev-sentry-deb-tether
# Captures the new sentry image hallmark. Cache should make this quick.

for np in srjcl pluml tadmor; do
  echo "=== $np ==="
  ./tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh "$np"
  git -c user.email=cerebro-local@local -c user.name=cerebro-local \
      add ".rbk/$np/rbrn.env"
  git -c user.email=cerebro-local@local -c user.name=cerebro-local \
      commit --no-verify -m "linux-local per-IP kludge $np"
done
```

### Step 3 — Sequential three-fixture verification

cerebro's BUK launcher serializes on `output-buk/current/`, so fixtures must run sequentially. Run srjcl first (decisive case is the regression target), then pluml, then tadmor.

```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"

./tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.srjcl.sh
# Expect: rbtdrc_srjcl_jupyter_running ✓, rbtdrc_srjcl_jupyter_connectivity ✓ (this is the prior-failure regression case), rbtdrc_srjcl_websocket_kernel ✓ → 3/3.

./tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.pluml.sh
# Expect: rbtdrc_pluml_text_rendering ✓ (also a curia→published-port probe), plus 4 more → 5/5.

./tt/rbtd-r.FixtureRun.tadmor.sh
# Expect: 54/54. The decisive cases are around position 30-32 in the run order:
#   rbtdrc_sortie_direct_sentry_probe        — enclave attacker dialing sentry:7999 must be BLOCKED
#   rbtdrc_sortie_net_dnat_entry_reflection  — same invariant via secondary check
#   rbtdrc_sortie_net_srcip_spoof            — existing spoof patterns still blocked
```

### Step 4 — Optional source-IP capture during srjcl run

If srjcl passes and you want to confirm what source IP the bottle's listener sees on linux (data point for cross-runtime structural catalog), re-charge srjcl, capture, single curl probe, quench:

```bash
./tt/rbw-cC.Charge.srjcl.sh

# In parallel: capture in both pentacle and sentry (tcpdump is now in the image)
docker exec canrbhm-srjcl-pentacle timeout 8 tcpdump -U -l -nn -i any -c 30 'tcp port 7999 or tcp port 8000' > /tmp/pen.txt 2>&1 &
docker exec canrbhm-srjcl-sentry   timeout 8 tcpdump -U -l -nn -i any -c 30 'tcp or icmp'                  > /tmp/sen.txt 2>&1 &
sleep 2
curl --connect-timeout 3 --max-time 5 -s -o /dev/null -w 'HTTP %{http_code}\n' http://localhost:7999/lab
sleep 3
wait

echo === PENTACLE; cat /tmp/pen.txt
echo === SENTRY  ; cat /tmp/sen.txt

# Sentry counter deltas
docker exec canrbhm-srjcl-sentry iptables -t nat -L PREROUTING -v -n
docker exec canrbhm-srjcl-sentry iptables -t nat -L POSTROUTING -v -n
docker exec canrbhm-srjcl-sentry iptables -L RBM-FORWARD -v -n

./tt/rbw-cQ.Quench.srjcl.sh
```

Skip if main verification is fast and complete.

## Reporting

When done, report:

1. **Platform identifier:** `docker info | head -5` output (engine version).
2. **Three-fixture outcomes:**
   - srjcl X/Y, naming any failed case
   - pluml X/Y, naming any failed case
   - tadmor X/Y, with explicit confirmation that `direct_sentry_probe`, `net_dnat_entry_reflection`, `net_srcip_spoof` passed (or which one failed)
3. **Source IP at bottle's listener** during the optional curl probe (from pentacle's tcpdump) — adds to the cross-runtime structural-fact catalog. Expected: probably `10.242.2.1` (the enclave bridge gateway, host-side) given prior cerebro data; we want to confirm the per-IP form lets it through.
4. **Sentry's PREROUTING DNAT counter** after a single successful curl probe — should be non-zero (was 0 under whole-CIDR).
5. **Any case that fails:** the case name, the failure surface (HTTP code, error message), your hypothesis. Per the architect's iteration discipline, do NOT improvise architectural fixes; report and let the architect decide the next step.

## Anti-patterns

- DO NOT modify the source tree on cerebro (the per-IP form is what we're testing as-is).
- DO NOT skip the vessel kludge — the new code needs to be in the running sentry image.
- DO NOT push the throwaway `linux-local per-IP kludge` commits back to origin.
- If anything other than the listed expectations surfaces, hold and report. The architect interprets and directs the next iteration.

done

content.text:
**Per-nameplate** — the field is `RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC` in each `.rbk/<moniker>/rbrn.env`.

| Nameplate | Delay |
|---|---|
| srjcl | 30s (Jupyter warmup) |
| pluml | 30s (PlantUML warmup) |
| tadmor | **0s** (adversarial, no service to warm) |
| moriah | 0s |
| ccyolo | 0s |

So tadmor's ~7:46 wall-clock is essentially all test execution (charge + 54 cases + quench), not readiness padding. The cases themselves are what take time — many of them are network probes with their own short timeouts.
